label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking whether following a set of instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: Take 3 steps. Take 7 steps. Turn around. Take 10 steps. The options are Yes or No. Let me break this down step by step. First, take 3 steps in a direction. Let's say I start at point A and take 3 steps forward to point B. Then take 7 steps. So from point B, I take 7 more steps forward to point C. Now I'm at point C, which is 3 + 7 = 10 steps from the starting point A. Then I turn around. If I was facing forward earlier, now I'm facing backward. So my direction is reversed. Finally, take 10 steps. Since I'm facing the opposite direction now, these 10 steps will take me back toward the starting point. From point C, 10 steps backward will bring me to point D. Now let's see where point D is in relation to the starting point A. I moved forward a total of 10 steps (3 + 7) to get to point C, then I moved back 10 steps. So 10 steps forward and 10 steps back would bring me back to the starting point A. So, yes, following these instructions should return me to the starting point. Wait, let me double-check.
